$description: Machine-readable disposition of Stryker survivors reviewed as behaviorally equivalent in the three critical formula modules (js/formula-parse.ts, js/formula-operand.ts, js/formula-ref.ts). This documentation never excludes or suppresses mutants: every survivor remains counted in Stryker reports. It records ONLY proof-quality equivalence arguments for mutants explicitly listed in `entries`. It does NOT claim a survivor is 'covered' or 'killed' by a new test unless an actual fresh (or targeted incremental) Stryker rerun shows that exact mutant's status flip to Killed — a test file merely targeting a survivor is not evidence and is never enough to disposition it here. socialcalcconstants.ts is intentionally outside this critical registry and remains in the full 11-module matrix with no mutator exclusions.
$schema_note: entries[].location uses Stryker's own 1-based line/column mutant location format. Stryker reassigns numeric mutant `id`s per run, so entries[].id is NOT stable across runs and must not be used to cross-reference a fresh mutation.json — match on (file, mutatorName, location, replacement) instead, which is stable for as long as the source line doesn't move. scripts/verify-mutation-disposition.mjs implements this exact matching and fails loudly on any Survived mutant in a fresh report that has no entry here.

reviewedAgainst.mutationJson: reports/mutation/critical/mutation.json (fresh run 2026-07-12 18:06; not checked in, regenerate via an isolated sandbox MUTATE_SCOPE=critical run — never in this working checkpoint, see the repo-level ABSOLUTE RULE against running Stryker here)

measuredCriticalScope.note: Fresh no-exclusion run after narrowing critical scope to exactly formula-parse.ts, formula-operand.ts, and formula-ref.ts; deterministic 23-file test subset.

reviewedAgainst.note: Fresh report has 72 survivors. 43 of them match a proof-quality equivalence entry in this registry (27 carried over plus 16 newly reviewed this session; a 44th entry, id=476, was survived when written but is now incidentally Killed in the fresh report — left in place as a still-valid equivalence proof, not removed). The remaining 29 fresh formula-module survivors are NOT dispositioned here: new boundary/error/type/reference tests in the three survivor test files target them, but no isolated-sandbox Stryker rerun has confirmed any of the 29 actually flips to Killed, so they are UNVERIFIED gaps, not 'covered' or 'resolved' — see undispositionedSurvivors below for the exact per-file breakdown pending a real rerun. This registry does not claim zero undispositioned survivors; scripts/verify-mutation-disposition.mjs recomputes and enforces this count directly from a fresh report.

undispositionedSurvivors.note: Exact counts of Survived mutants with no matching entries[] tuple. The three formula-module counts below are freshly recomputed from the 2026-07-12 18:06 critical report (reports/mutation/critical/mutation.json) via scripts/verify-mutation-disposition.mjs and are real, currently-open gaps pending an isolated-sandbox rerun after the new survivor tests are verified to actually kill them — NOT claimed as covered. socialcalcconstants.ts is a separate, larger figure carried over from the full 11-module matrix (out of critical-registry scope) and is not recomputed by that script. Updated 2026-07-13: 3 formula-ref L370 lockstep equivalents (ids 1364/1365/1368 style) added to entries[]; formula-ref per-file report now 13/13 accounted.

entries.justification: Every `if (state == X)` block in ParseFormulaIntoTokens is a bare `if`, not `else if`, so a state change made here lets the SAME character cascade into the coord-state block within the same loop iteration. Forcing this incoord check to always transition to `state = coord` (instead of only for the literal '$' char) is unobservable because: (1) `str` accumulated while in the alpha state can never contain a digit (a digit transitions out of alpha before being appended), so `coordregex` (which requires a trailing digit) can never match, meaning the coord-state handler's op/numstart/space/eof branch always falls back to pushing the same `name` token the alpha branch would have pushed directly; and (2) the coord-state error branch pushes the identical `s_parseerrchar` text the alpha branch's error branch would have pushed. An exhaustive differential test of every printable-ASCII 2-char suffix after 'A' and '$', comparing the real function against a mutated clone, found zero distinguishing input.

charclass.numstart (2) is assigned to exactly one character in CharClassTable: '.'. The alpha state's own second branch (`cclass == charclass.alpha || ch == "."`) already intercepts every '.' character before this terminator group is ever reached, so `cclass == charclass.numstart` can never be observed true (nor can flipping it to != ever be observed false in a way that changes control flow) at this program point. The clause is provably dead code given the fixed CharClassTable content.

This else-block (`last_token_type = charclass.eof; last_token_text = "EOF";`) only executes when `parseinfo.length == 0`. The only subsequent read of these two variables is the unary-operator check `parseinfo.length == 0 || (last_token_type == charclass.op && ...)`, whose left operand is already true in this exact case, so `||` short-circuits and the right-hand operand (the only place these variables are read) is never evaluated. Emptying the block's body therefore has zero observable effect on any token stream.

The space branch's body (`{ continue; }`) is the LAST statement inside the for-loop body in ConvertInfixToPolish (nothing follows the if/else-if chain before the loop's closing brace), so replacing it with an empty block is behaviorally identical: falling off the end of the loop body reaches the next iteration exactly the way `continue` does. Verified by feeding a hand-built `[num, space, num]` token array through the real function and a mutated clone: both return the identical `[0, 2]`.

SC.Formula.TokenPrecedence's full value set is {1,2,-3,4,5,6,7,8,9} — no opcode ever has precedence exactly 0, and an opcode lookup miss yields `undefined`, for which `>= 0` and `> 0` are both `false`. So `tprecedence >= 0` and `tprecedence > 0` can only ever disagree at exactly 0, which is unreachable for any real opcode drawn from that fixed, hardcoded table. An 8000-formula randomized differential fuzz against a hand-transcribed reference implementation of ConvertInfixToPolish found zero disagreements.

Same reasoning as id=843: tprecedence is drawn from the fixed TokenPrecedence table {1,2,-3,4,5,6,7,8,9} (or is `undefined` on a miss), which never contains exactly 0, so `< 0` and `<= 0` are provably identical for every reachable value. Confirmed with the same 8000-formula differential fuzz.

Identical reasoning to id=850, applied to tstackprecedence instead of tprecedence — both are values looked up from the same fixed, zero-free TokenPrecedence table, so `< 0` and `<= 0` are indistinguishable for every reachable input. Confirmed with the same 8000-formula differential fuzz.

FormulaTokenTypeCode is a closed TypeScript union `1|2|3|4|5|6|7` (num/coord/op/name/error/string/space). In the ttype/ttext if-else-if chain this condition terminates, every other legal value is already consumed by an earlier branch: num/coord/string by the first branch, name by the second, space by the third, and the ttext-based comma/'('/')' checks plus the generic `ttype == tokentype.op` branch cover every op-typed token. By the time this final `else if` is reached, `ttype` can only legally be `tokentype.error` (5) given the type system, so the condition can never be false there — forcing it to `true` changes nothing observable.

`pos2 = table1.indexOf("|", pos1 + 1)` always searches starting at `pos1 + 1`, and this line is only reached inside `if (pos1 >= 0)`, so the search's fromIndex is always >= 1. String.indexOf can never return exactly 0 when searching from an index >= 1, so a found pos2 is always >= 1, and a miss is always -1 — 0 is unreachable. `pos2 < 0` and `pos2 <= 0` are therefore provably identical for every value this variable can hold.

Identical reasoning to id=905, applied to the second (wildcard type2) lookup block, which is likewise only reached inside `if (pos1 >= 0)` with the same `table1.indexOf("|", pos1 + 1)` fromIndex >= 1 guarantee — pos2 can never be exactly 0.

OperandValueAndType L184-186 else branch assigns cellvtype='b' when the coord's cell is missing. The very next read of cellvtype is L187 `result.type = cellvtype || 'b'`. When the else runs, cellvtype is undefined (else block emptied) OR 'b' (original); `undefined || 'b'` === `'b' || 'b'` === 'b'. The `||` fallback masks the difference for every reachable input (cell missing => cellvtype unassigned; cell present => else not entered). No test can observe a difference.

OperandAsType `var result = { type: '', value: '' }` at L222. Every return path either (a) fully overwrites result.type and result.value before returning (empty-stacklen path L228-229, non-empty path L234-235, type-mismatch path L244-245), or (b) replaces `result` entirely via `result = LookupName(...)` at L238 then overwrites on the subsequent branches. No path reads result.type/result.value before assignment. Mutating the initial literal to {} is unobservable.

OperandsAsCoordOnSheet `var value1 = { type: '', value: '' }` at L257. L262-263 unconditionally assign value1.value and value1.type from operand[stacklen-1] before any read or return. The initial literal's type/value fields are dead; {} vs {type:'',value:''} is unobservable.

OperandsAsCoordOnSheet `var result = { type: '', value: '' }` at L258. Every return path fully overwrites result.type and result.value (L271-272 / L274-275 / L285-287 / L294 then L297/L304/L306/L309-310) or sets result.error only alongside an already-set type/value. No path returns result with the initial empty-string fields still in place. Mutating to {} is unobservable.

OperandsAsRangeOnSheet `var value2 = { type: '', value: '' }` at L323. L328-329 unconditionally assign value2.value and value2.type from operand[stacklen-1] before any return. Initial literal fields are dead; {} is unobservable.

OperandsAsRangeOnSheet L343 `pos2 = leftCoord.indexOf('|', pos1 + 1)`. This line is only reached when `pos1 != -1` (L341), so pos1 >= 0 and the indexOf search starts at pos1+1 >= 1. String.prototype.indexOf can only return 0 when searching from a start index of 0; searching from >= 1 can never find a match at index 0. Therefore pos2 is either -1 (no match) or >= 1 (match found). `pos2 < 0` and `pos2 <= 0` are identical over the reachable set {-1} ∪ [1, ∞); the value 0 is unreachable. The mutants are behaviorally identical.

OperandAsSheetName `var result = { type: '', value: '' }` at L381. L385-386 unconditionally assign result.value and result.type from operand[stacklen-1] before any return or read. Initial literal fields are dead; {} is unobservable.

rcColname's clamp `if (c > 702) c = 702` and the mutant `if (c >= 702) c = 702` produce an identical resulting `c` for every input: for c<702 neither fires (same c); for c>702 both fire and clamp to 702 (same c); at exactly c=702 the original doesn't fire (c stays 702) while the mutant fires but assigns c=702 to a variable already equal to 702 — a no-op. The clamp target equals the comparison threshold, so strict vs non-strict inequality is unobservable for all inputs.
Same clamp fixed-point argument as id=976, mirrored at the lower bound: `if (c < 1) c = 1` vs `if (c <= 1) c = 1` are identical for all c because clamping c=1 to 1 is a no-op.
crToCoord's column lower-bound clamp; identical fixed-point argument as id=980 (clamp target 1 equals threshold 1).
crToCoord's column upper-bound clamp; identical fixed-point argument as id=976 (clamp target 702 equals threshold 702).
crToCoord's row lower-bound clamp; identical fixed-point argument as id=980, applied to r instead of c.

The mutated loop runs one extra iteration at i===cr.length. String.charCodeAt(len) is spec-guaranteed to return NaN for any string. Every comparison in the loop body (ch===36, ch<=57, ch>=97, ch>=65) is false for NaN by IEEE-754 semantics, so none of the four branches execute and neither `c` nor `r` changes. The extra iteration is a provable no-op for every possible input string, including the empty string.

AdjustFormulaCoords deleted-band check `(coloffset<0 && cr.col>=col && cr.col<col-coloffset)`. Forcing the first conjunct to `true` reduces it to `cr.col>=col && cr.col<col-coloffset`. When coloffset>=0, col-coloffset<=col, so the interval [col, col-coloffset) is always empty (upper bound never exceeds the lower bound) — the reduced expression is provably false for every cr.col, matching the original (which is also false whenever coloffset>=0 via its own first conjunct). When coloffset<0, the original's first conjunct is already true, so both expressions reduce identically. The two are behaviorally identical for all (coloffset, col, cr.col).

Same range-emptiness argument as id=1297, extended to the coloffset===0 boundary: at coloffset=0, col-coloffset=col, so the interval [col,col) is empty regardless of the conjunct's truth value. The threshold shift from < to <= is therefore unobservable for every input.
Row-clause mirror of id=1297: `(rowoffset<0 && cr.row>=row && cr.row<row-rowoffset)` — identical range-emptiness argument applied to row/rowoffset instead of col/coloffset.
Row-clause mirror of id=1298: identical boundary-emptiness argument at rowoffset===0.

Identical reasoning to id=476: charclass.numstart (2) is assigned to exactly one character in CharClassTable: '.'. The alpha state's own second branch (`cclass == charclass.alpha || ch == "."`) already intercepts every '.' character before this terminator group is ever reached, so `cclass == charclass.numstart` can never be observed true at this program point. Forcing it to `false` (removing the clause from the OR group) changes nothing for any reachable input.

When a coordinate points to a missing cell, this literal is assigned to cellvtype and immediately normalized by `result.type = cellvtype || "b"`; replacing "b" with the empty string therefore still selects the same fallback "b" for every reachable missing-cell path.
OperandAsType returns immediately with e#REF!/0 on an empty stack or overwrites both fields from the popped operand before any return; its initial type literal is never observed.
OperandAsType returns immediately with e#REF!/0 on an empty stack or overwrites both fields from the popped operand before any return; its initial value literal is never observed.
OperandsAsCoordOnSheet overwrites value1.type from the popped operand at L263 before any read or return; the initial type literal cannot reach any return.
OperandsAsCoordOnSheet overwrites value1.value from the popped operand at L262 before any read or return; the initial value literal cannot reach any return.
OperandsAsCoordOnSheet's result initializer is fully overwritten on every return path (type/value at L271-275, L285-287, L294-310); replacing the initial type literal is unobservable.
OperandsAsCoordOnSheet's result initializer is fully overwritten on every return path (type/value at L271-275, L285-287, L294-310); replacing the initial value literal is unobservable.
OperandsAsRangeOnSheet overwrites value2.type from the popped operand at L329 before any read or return; the initial type literal cannot reach any return.
OperandsAsRangeOnSheet overwrites value2.value from the popped operand at L328 before any read or return; the initial value literal cannot reach any return.
OperandAsSheetName overwrites result.type from the popped operand at L386 before any branch can observe the initializer; the initial type literal cannot reach any return.
OperandAsSheetName overwrites result.value from the popped operand at L385 before any branch can observe the initializer; the initial value literal cannot reach any return.

For a missing coordinate cell, this value is assigned and then immediately overwritten by the final non-text branch with the empty string before return; replacing the earlier assignment is unobservable.
ParseFormulaIntoTokens assigns str from the first character before any read on every non-empty input; for empty input it is never read, so replacing its initializer cannot affect output.
twochrop is assigned from last_token_text + str immediately before every read; its initializer is never observed.

At EOF, ch is only used in branches selected by cclass; every EOF branch either ignores ch or takes a fixed error/token path without reading its value, so replacing the EOF initializer is unobservable.

ParseFormulaIntoTokens assigns last_token_text = "EOF" only when parseinfo is empty. The immediately following condition short-circuits on parseinfo.length == 0, and the only later read in the unary ')' check is false for the first operator because last_token_text is not "("; replacing "EOF" with "" is therefore unobservable.

AdjustFormulaCoords zeros cr.col and cr.row TOGETHER (both set to 0 in one unconditional block at L346-349) whenever the coord falls in a deletion band. The subsequent col/row offset applications (L352-357) only execute when !sheetref and can never drive a non-deleted coord below 1 (col>=1 from coordToCr, offset>=0 for insertions; deletions already zeroed both). Sheet-qualified refs skip both blocks entirely, leaving cr.col/cr.row as coordToCr output which is always >=1 for any token the lexer tags coord. Therefore cr.row<1 and cr.col<1 are always in lockstep: both true (deletion zeroed both) or both false (not deleted). Replacing || with && at L370 is equivalent because whenever one disjunct is true, the other is also true — the disjunction truth value is unchanged by this operator mutation.

Forcing cr.row<1 to false at L370 is equivalent: cr.row<1 is true only after the deletion-band zeroing block (L346-349), which also sets cr.col=0 (cr.col<1=true). The surviving disjunct cr.col<1 remains true in every reachable case where cr.row<1 was true — and the third disjunct cr.col>702 handles column overflow independently — so the shortened disjunction (false || cr.col<1 || cr.col>702) is always equal to the original (cr.row<1 || cr.col<1 || cr.col>702). See id=1364 for the lockstep proof.

Forcing cr.col<1 to false at L370 is equivalent: cr.col<1 is true only after the deletion-band zeroing block (L346-349), which also sets cr.row=0 (cr.row<1=true). The surviving disjunct cr.row<1 remains true in every reachable case where cr.col<1 was true, so (cr.row<1 || false || cr.col>702) equals the original. See id=1364 for the lockstep proof.
